paper_title: NGS Data Analysis and Sampling in Norwegian Fish Farming Sites

methods.description: This method involves assembling, quality-filtering, and de-multiplexing raw sequence data from Illumina sequencing platforms to analyze diversity data. It includes filtering cross-contamination, dereplication, taxonomy assignment, and OTU clustering.

steps.description: We assembled, quality-filtered and de-multiplexed the raw sequence data using a computational pipeline specifically tailored for analysing diversity data generated by Illumina sequencing platforms.
steps.searchable_summary: assemble and quality-filter raw sequence data from Illumina platforms

steps.description: We filtered cross-contamination events that stem from the library-preparation artefact referred to as the mistagging phenomenon.
steps.searchable_summary: filter cross-contamination from mistagging in sequence data

steps.description: After assembly of the paired-end sequencing reads into contiguous sequences, we only kept the unique copies of these sequences (strict dereplication).
steps.searchable_summary: perform strict dereplication on assembled sequences

steps.description: We removed the unique sequences with a single occurrence in a library sample and compared them for each pair of samples corresponding to PCR duplicates pooled in separate libraries.
steps.searchable_summary: remove single occurrence sequences and compare duplicates

steps.description: We then assigned taxonomies to unique sequences based on the Needleman-Wunsch global alignments.
steps.searchable_summary: assign taxonomies using Needleman-Wunsch global alignments

steps.description: We grouped the unique sequences based on their taxonomies to create large pre-clusters of sequences that we divided into operational taxonomic units (OTUs).
steps.searchable_summary: group sequences into OTUs based on taxonomy

steps.description: We performed a normalization of the OTUs-to-samples dataset using a rarefaction approach.

methods.description: This method describes the collection of sediment samples from fish farming sites in Norway, including procedures for sample preservation, storage, and initial analysis.

steps.description: The samples were collected in 4 fish farming sites situated in Norway, in the coastal regions 'West' and 'Central'.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment samples from fish farming sites in Norway

steps.description: Each sample was placed in a tube containing 5 ml of Life Guard Soil Preservation Solution.
steps.searchable_summary: preserve sediment samples with Life Guard Soil Preservation Solution

steps.description: The samples were stored in a cooler at 4°C and then frozen at −20°C after returning to the laboratory.
steps.searchable_summary: store and freeze sediment samples for preservation

steps.description: For each sediment sample, 2 measurements of redox potentials were taken with a probe.
steps.searchable_summary: measure redox potentials of sediment samples

steps.description: Additional surface sediment material was sub-sampled for morphological analyses of foraminiferal communities.
